phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: Slight edge at level 1: your wave-dash blade stacks beat Anivia's wall-frost poke control on the margins. Take the short trades you start — just don't force a coin-flip all-in off a small lead.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: At level 2 the numbers lean your way — wave-dash blade stacks edge out the wall-frost poke control when both sides play it straight. Press the small advantages: wave position, first poke, cooldown ticks.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: Your wave-dash blade stacks out-muscle Anivia's wall-frost poke control at level 3 — this window is yours, so force the trades and tax every step toward the wave.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Through levels 4-5 your wave-dash blade stacks beat the wall-frost poke control in a straight exchange. Play the front foot — make Anivia answer you, not the other way around.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: Slight deficit at the level-6 breakpoint: Anivia's wall-frost poke control edge out your wave-dash blade stacks on the margins. You can still trade — just let him start the exchanges and answer on your terms.

phases.label: First item
phases.why: On first item the numbers lean his way — the wall-frost poke control out-tick your wave-dash blade stacks in even exchanges. Don't bleed for pride; take the trades his cooldowns hand you.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: Slight deficit at two-plus items: Anivia's wall-frost poke control edge out your wave-dash blade stacks on the margins. You can still trade — just let him start the exchanges and answer on your terms.

phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: Q the low minions and keep the blade moving — your level 1 reset dance already out-trades careless mages.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: W joins: the channel shreds incoming damage — hold it for his trade spell, not his autos.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: E completes the engine: the stun into stacked-passive autos is half a health bar at 3. Mid lane rarely budgets for it.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Reset-dance the wave into his face: four stacks, E-stun, Q the kill threat. The lane is a minion-count quiz.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: Vanguard's Edge online — the all-in caps with a wall of disengage-proof marks. Dive with the wave as your fuel.

phases.label: First item
phases.why: First item spike: stacked trades delete mages and your sustain mocks their poke math.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: At two-plus items the numbers lean his way — the stardust ramp control out-tick your wave-dash blade stacks in even exchanges. Don't bleed for pride; take the trades his cooldowns hand you.

phases.why: First item spike: stacked trades delete mages and your sustain mocks their poke math.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: Late Irelia surfs the fight: Q-reset through their line, stun the carry pair, and shred whoever peels.

phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: Q the low minions and keep the blade moving — your level 1 reset dance already out-trades careless mages.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: W joins: the channel shreds incoming damage — hold it for his trade spell, not his autos.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: E completes the engine: the stun into stacked-passive autos is half a health bar at 3. Mid lane rarely budgets for it.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Reset-dance the wave into his face: four stacks, E-stun, Q the kill threat. The lane is a minion-count quiz.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: Vanguard's Edge online — the all-in caps with a wall of disengage-proof marks. Dive with the wave as your fuel.

phases.why: Slight deficit on first item: Yasuo's windwall dash-weaving edge out your wave-dash blade stacks on the margins. You can still trade — just let him start the exchanges and answer on your terms.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: At two-plus items the numbers lean his way — the windwall dash-weaving out-tick your wave-dash blade stacks in even exchanges. Don't bleed for pride; take the trades his cooldowns hand you.

phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: Q the low minions and keep the blade moving — your level 1 reset dance already out-trades careless mages.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: Slight edge at level 2: your wave-dash blade stacks beat Yone's double-blade spirit dives on the margins. Take the short trades you start — just don't force a coin-flip all-in off a small lead.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: At level 3 the numbers lean your way — wave-dash blade stacks edge out the double-blade spirit dives when both sides play it straight. Press the small advantages: wave position, first poke, cooldown ticks.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Slight edge through levels 4-5: your wave-dash blade stacks beat Yone's double-blade spirit dives on the margins. Take the short trades you start — just don't force a coin-flip all-in off a small lead.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: At the level-6 breakpoint the numbers lean your way — wave-dash blade stacks edge out the double-blade spirit dives when both sides play it straight. Press the small advantages: wave position, first poke, cooldown ticks.

phases.label: First item
phases.why: Slight deficit on first item: Yone's double-blade spirit dives edge out your wave-dash blade stacks on the margins. You can still trade — just let him start the exchanges and answer on your terms.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: At two-plus items the numbers lean his way — the double-blade spirit dives out-tick your wave-dash blade stacks in even exchanges. Don't bleed for pride; take the trades his cooldowns hand you.

phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: Q the low minions and keep the blade moving — your level 1 reset dance already out-trades careless mages.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: W joins: the channel shreds incoming damage — hold it for his trade spell, not his autos.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: E completes the engine: the stun into stacked-passive autos is half a health bar at 3. Mid lane rarely budgets for it.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Reset-dance the wave into his face: four stacks, E-stun, Q the kill threat. The lane is a minion-count quiz.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: Vanguard's Edge online — the all-in caps with a wall of disengage-proof marks. Dive with the wave as your fuel.

phases.label: First item
phases.why: First item spike: stacked trades delete mages and your sustain mocks their poke math.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: Late Irelia surfs the fight: Q-reset through their line, stun the carry pair, and shred whoever peels.
